changes_made: Renamed k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted to k8s_service_loadbalancer_ip_restricted for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to boundary protection. NetworkPolicies are correctly used to enforce ingress and egress restrictions. The function for LoadBalancer services was renamed for clarity to specify that IP restrictions are the focus. TLS for Ingress is correctly identified as a necessary security measure.
naming_improvements: Changed 'k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted' to 'k8s_service_loadbalancer_ip_restricted' to better describe the specific check being performed.
title: Boundary Protection - Boundary protection | public access [withdrawn
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7(2) focuses on boundary protection and public access restrictions. In Kubernetes, similar protections can be enforced using NetworkPolicies to restrict ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that no traffic is allowed by default unless explicitly permitted. Additionally, services exposed via LoadBalancer should be restricted to specific IP ranges to prevent unrestricted public access. Ingress resources should have TLS enabled to secure traffic.
